back_translation: The LORD appeared to Abraham at the oaks of Mamre, in the heat of the day, while Abraham was sitting at the entrance of his tent.
Abraham looked up and saw, behold, three men standing nearby. When he saw them, he ran from the entrance of his tent to greet them and bowed low to the ground.
Abraham said, "My lord, if I have found favor in your sight, please do not pass by your servant.
Please let me bring a little water so you may wash your feet and rest under this tree.
Let me bring a bit of bread so you may be refreshed, then continue your journey, since you have visited your servant." They replied, "Do as you have said."
Abraham hurried into the tent to Sarah and said, "Quickly, take three seahs of fine flour, knead it, and bake some bread."
Abraham ran to the herd, chose a tender and good calf, and gave it to a servant to prepare quickly.
Abraham brought curds, milk, and the prepared calf and set them before the men, and stood near them under the tree as they ate.
They asked Abraham, "Where is Sarah your wife?" He answered, "There in the tent."
He said, "I will surely return to you at this time next year, and Sarah your wife will have a son." Sarah was behind Abraham, listening at the tent entrance.
Abraham and Sarah were already old, advanced in years; Sarah had passed the age of childbearing.
So Sarah laughed within herself, saying, "After I have grown decrepit, will I now have pleasure? My master is also old."
The LORD said to Abraham, "Why did Sarah laugh, saying, 'Can I really bear a child when I am old?'
Is anything too difficult for the LORD? At the appointed time I will return to you, at this time next year, and Sarah will have a son."
Sarah was afraid and denied it, saying, "I did not laugh." But He said, "No, you did laugh."
When the men got up to leave, they looked toward Sodom, and Abraham walked along with them to send them off.
The LORD said, "Shall I hide from Abraham what I am about to do?
Abraham will surely become a great and powerful nation, and all nations of the earth will be blessed through him.

For I have chosen him, so that he may instruct his children and household after him to keep the way of the LORD by doing righteousness and justice, in order that the LORD may bring upon Abraham what He has promised."
The LORD said, "The outcry against Sodom and Gomorrah is great, and their sin is exceedingly grievous.
I will go down to see whether they have acted altogether according to the outcry that has reached Me. If not, I will know."
The two men turned away from there and went toward Sodom, but Abraham remained standing before the LORD.
Abraham approached and said, "Will You really sweep away the righteous along with the wicked?
Suppose there are 50 righteous people in the city. Will You really sweep it away and not spare the place for the sake of the 50 righteous within it?
Far be it from You to do such a thing—to kill the righteous with the wicked, treating the righteous like the wicked. Far be it from You! Will not the Judge of all the earth do justly?"
The LORD said, "If I find 50 righteous in the city of Sodom, I will spare the whole place for their sake."
Abraham answered, "Now I have ventured to speak to the Lord, though I am only dust and ashes.
Suppose the 50 righteous lack 5—will You destroy the whole city for the lack of 5?" He said, "I will not destroy it if I find 45 there."
Abraham spoke to Him again, "Suppose 40 are found there?" He said, "For the sake of the 40, I will not do it."
Abraham said, "May the Lord not be angry, but let me speak. Suppose 30 are found there?" He said, "I will not do it if I find 30 there."
Abraham said, "Now I have ventured to speak to the Lord. Suppose 20 are found there?" He said, "I will not destroy it for the sake of the 20."
Abraham said, "May the Lord not be angry; let me speak just once more. Suppose 10 are found there?" He said, "I will not destroy it for the sake of the 10."
When the LORD had finished speaking with Abraham, He departed, and Abraham returned to his place.
